study_group_representative.quote: Between 10 January and 24 January (period until confirmation of the first cases in France), nine possible cases were identified in France; among them, three cases were confirmed with COVID-19.

intervention_and_participants_defined.quote: Here we describe the real-time implementation of this surveillance scheme for the first three imported cases of COVID-19 identified in France, who were confirmed on 24 January 2020 in persons with a recent stay in Wuhan. ... We present data until 12 February on the follow-up of the cases’ contacts initiated immediately upon confirmation of infection.

outcome_assessed_for_60pct.quote: As at 12 February, the contacts of the three first confirmed cases of COVID-19 in France have been followed up for the whole 14 days follow-up time after the cases isolation.

follow_up_length_reported.quote: As at 12 February, the contacts of the three first confirmed cases of COVID-19 in France have been followed up for the whole 14 days follow-up time after the cases isolation.

outcome_assessors_blinded.quote: The text does not provide information regarding whether the outcome assessors were blinded to the investigated determinant.

outcome_definition_objective_precise.quote: A confirmed case was defined as a possible case with a positive SARS-CoV-2 RT-PCR on respiratory samples, performed by an accredited laboratory.

important_prognostic_factors_accounted_f.quote: Three levels of risk of infection are defined for contacts/co-exposed persons of a possible/confirmed COVID-19 case (Table). ... As at 12 February, the contacts of the three first confirmed cases of COVID-19 in France have been followed up for the whole 14 days follow-up time after the cases isolation.

analysis_described_and_effect_quantified.quote: Five contacts were evaluated as at low risk of exposure and 18 at moderate/high risk. ... no secondary transmission has been identified.
